name: axum (Ergonomic Async Rust Web Framework)

auth.notes: Web framework; integrate tower-based auth middleware

performance.notes: Top-tier async Rust performance; comparable to Actix-Web

agent_readiness.notes: Tokio-native async Rust web framework. Router::new().route('/path', get(handler)). Handler functions via extractor pattern (Path<T>, Query<T>, Json<T>, State<T>). No macros (unlike Actix-Web). Tower middleware ecosystem. WebSockets, SSE, streaming. tokio + hyper under the hood. Type-safe extractors infer types from handler args. Growing ecosystem. Preferred by many over Actix-Web for ergonomics. Used with sqlx, SeaORM, Diesel for full stack Rust APIs.

gotchas: Steep Rust learning curve (lifetimes, async, extractors)
Tower middleware can be complex to configure
Error handling requires careful type design
best_when: Production Rust HTTP services needing safety and performance
Teams wanting ergonomic async Rust over Actix-Web macros
Services integrating with Tokio ecosystem (sqlx, reqwest)
WebSocket or SSE endpoints in Rust
avoid_when: Teams not committed to Rust ecosystem
Need maximum raw performance (Actix-Web marginally faster)
summary: axum is the Tokio team's ergonomic async Rust web framework. Extractor pattern for type-safe handler args (Path, Query, Json, State). No macros. Tower middleware ecosystem. WebSockets, SSE, streaming. hyper + tokio underneath. Preferred by many over Actix-Web for ergonomics. Pre-v1 but production-ready. Pairs with sqlx/Diesel/SeaORM for full-stack Rust APIs.
